chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH AT KARACHI CP D 2797 of 2024 CP D 2798 of 2024 CP D 2799 of 2024 CP D 2800 of 2024 ____________________________________________________________ DATE ORDER WITH SIGNATURE OF JUDGE ____________________________________________________________ For orders as to maintainability of petition 18.03.2025 Mr. Subhan Javed, advocate for the petitioner Mr. Sandeep Malani, Assistant Advocate General Sindh along with DSP Ayaz Rajpar These four petitions have been filed with respect to the recruitment process of Police Constables said to have been concluded in the year 2022. The petitioners state that they not have been successful, hence, this petition. At the very onset it is noted that while the process is said to have been concluded in the year 2022 these petitions have been filed in 2024. Learned counsel remained unable to dispel bar of laches. Even otherwise orders are sought for employment which in fact would have a necessary effect of displacing those already appointed. Irrespective hereof not a single candidate has been arrayed as respondent herein. Allegations and assertions contained in the petition are factual in nature and could not be addressed without resort to inquiry, investigation and evidence etc. and the same is not amenable for adjudication in writ jurisdiction. These petitions, listed from the very onset for orders as to maintainability, are hereby dismissed. Office is instructed to place copy of this order in connected matters. Judge Judge Amjad
